English Recap:
Actually there were 3 attacks by Chinese people today
In the morning a group of local people mainly
We gathered, sit down protesting against CCP
We want to free HK and Muslim
and suddenly one of the Chinese guy just came out and punch the organizer, which shock everyone
We heard national anthem of chinese people playing
After that, they all singing Chinese national anthem over and over again
Our side, Australian and some Hong Kong people regathered, like two camps opposing each other
There was kind of chaotic, we were yelling each other,
we kept chanting free HK free Muslim that kind of stuff
and they said something like "China's HK", I don't know what that means)
after that a second attack happen , one of the Chinese people came out of the crowd threw a cup of orange juice on us and went away,
then a security came in and got him, some of us, Hongkonger also chase that chinese guy on
Because security were separating us, the situation were less tense, it went more peaceful
We Hongkonger went back to "grass floor" and we just kept singing "Sing Hallelujah to Lord "
Everything was peaceful
And suddenly the Chinese people gather and put their Chinese memo on our Lennon Wall,
saying like 中國加油, Hong Kong is part of chinese, we didn't really care because they have their right to write anything on it actually
Everything was peaceful and suddenly, a Chinese guy came up again and he destructed our posters
Originally there were some posters on the floor and that guy just threw them away
Some Hongkongers are holding posters here as well and he just tried to take away the posters from us, which was very rude
The Chinese guy came and attacked one of us and grabbed his neck

(The recap was interrupted here)

http://ppl.app.uq.edu.au/content/1.70.06-discrimination-and-harassment

今日比人圍嘅同學可以睇下呢條link
Attribute - one of the attributes contained within Commonwealth and State legislation:

sex
relationship status
pregnancy, or potential pregnancy
parental status
breastfeeding
age
race, including colour, national extraction, nationality, national or ethnic origin and immigration status
disability or impairment
religious belief or religious activity
political belief or activity
trade union activity
lawful sexual activity
gender identity
sexuality
intersex status
social origin
criminal record
family or carer's responsibilities
association with, or relation to, a person identified on the basis of any of the above attributes.



Harassment and/or bullying - behaviour towards an individual or group of individuals, that may or may not be based on the attributes referred to the Anti-Discrimination Act 1991 (Qld) and could be defined as "the repeated less favourable treatment of a person by another or others in the workplace, which may be considered unreasonable and inappropriate workplace practice. It includes behaviour that intimidates, offends, degrades or humiliates …" in circumstances where a reasonable person would have anticipated the possibility that the other person would be offended, humiliated or intimidated by the conduct”.

Harassment/bullying often involves a misuse of power. Harassment/bullying may occur between people of any gender. It can also occur between supervisor and staff, co-workers, students and between students and staff.

Harassment/bullying may be subtle or overt and includes, but is not limited to, the following forms of behaviour:

• Abusive and offensive language or shouting,
• Constant unreasonable criticism about work or academic performance, often about petty or insignificant matters,
• Deliberate exclusion, isolation or alienation of a staff member or student,
• Allocation of humiliating or demeaning tasks, or sabotaging a person's work,
• Setting of impossible deadlines with unrealistic expectations of work,
• Spreading gossip or false and malicious rumours with an intent to cause harm to a person,
• Sarcasm or ridicule,
• Threatening gestures or actual violence,
• Inappropriate comments about personal appearance,
• Electronic harassment such as through email, SMS,
• Hazing or bastardisation (such as harmful or humiliating initiation rituals).
推呀

一開始風平浪靜，但過不久，
中國人持著言論自由，愛香港，強姦築起的「連儂牆」
寫上愛中國，愛香港，愛一國兩制等等
中國人其後吹雞
香港學生和平對抗，唱Sing Hallelujah to Lord
中國學生反擊唱國歌，但要睇歌詞
其後他們不滿情緒升溫，拆牆, 踢display
雙方對峙中
香港人50-70 vs 中國人 200+
香港學生說如果佢哋散我哋就會散因為為保自身安全，而他們(中國)情緒激動，要香港學生收起poster 才散
